findings.title: Near-duplicate LLM and Embedding store modules (~90% identical)
findings.description: packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/llm.ts and packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/embedding.ts are ~90% identical. The only differences are: (1) localStorage key prefix ('settings/llm/' vs 'settings/embedding/'), (2) event names ('llm:config:update' vs 'embedding:config:update'), (3) ref name (llmConfigured vs embeddingConfigured), and (4) exported function name (sendLlmConfig vs sendEmbeddingConfig). The sendConfig functions at lines 21-56 in both files are structurally identical, including the same apiKey/baseURL extraction logic from config. This is a 10+ line near-duplicate block spanning the entire file structure.

findings.title: Triplicated apiKey/baseURL extraction from provider config
findings.code_snippet:
const apiKey = (config.apiKey ?? config['api-key'] ?? '') as string
const baseURL = (config.baseUrl ?? config['base-url'] ?? '') as string
findings.description: The exact same 2-line pattern for extracting apiKey and baseURL from a provider config appears in three separate files: packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/llm.ts (line 37-38), packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/embedding.ts (line 37-38), and packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/memory.ts (line 104-105). This dual-key lookup ('apiKey' vs 'api-key', 'baseUrl' vs 'base-url') is a form of defensive coding against inconsistent provider config shape, and the 3-site repetition meets the extraction threshold.

findings.title: Near-duplicate LLM and Embedding backend handler modules
findings.description: services/airi-brain/src/handlers/llm.ts and services/airi-brain/src/handlers/embedding.ts share nearly identical structure for their first ~55 lines. Both have: (1) a pushStatus() function that checks the same 4 fields (provider, apiKey, baseURL, model), (2) a registerHandler that loads persisted config, applies it if complete, listens for config:update, persists new config, creates a handle, and pushes status. The 4-field completeness check `!!(config.provider && config.apiKey && config.baseURL && config.model)` appears 4 times across both files. The embedding handler has extra model-listing/validation logic, but the core config management is duplicated.

findings.title: Repeated try/catch error message extraction pattern (6+ occurrences)
findings.description: The pattern `const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` appears 6+ times across the brain service files: pipeline.ts (lines 58, 73, 93), index.ts (line 156, 166), evening-pipeline.ts (line 108), embedding.ts (line 118, 169). Each time it extracts a string message from an unknown error. While individually simple, this is a pervasive micro-duplication.

findings.title: createPipeline has 3 near-identical try/catch blocks for component init
findings.description: The createPipeline function (lines 30-102, 72 lines) contains three structurally identical blocks: each checks adapter availability, dynamically imports a class from context-engine, instantiates it, logs success, catches errors with the same pattern, and logs a skip message in the else branch. This is a clear case of copy-paste-modify across three pipeline components (MemoryOrchestrator, ReportGenerator, MemoryExtractor) spanning lines 46-99.

findings.title: autoEnableModules has 8 identical try/catch blocks
findings.description: The autoEnableModules function (lines 65-133, 68 lines) contains 8 structurally identical try/catch blocks. Each block calls a store's initialize(), pushes the module name to an array, and catches with console.error. This is the most obvious copy-paste pattern in the diff and accounts for the function exceeding the 50-line threshold.

findings.title: createLlmHandle and createEmbeddingHandle are identical functions
findings.description: createEmbeddingHandle (lines 120-131) and createLlmHandle (lines 137-148) in providers.ts are structurally identical. They both spread config, log a message, and return an object with config and requestDefaults(). The only difference is the log message text. The LlmConfig and EmbeddingConfig interfaces (lines 7-19) are also identical in shape (provider, apiKey, baseURL, model). Additionally, the same handle-creation pattern appears inline in createBrainProviders (lines 78-85, 102-109), making this a 4-site duplication.

findings.title: Duplicated normalizeBaseUrl function across frontend providers
findings.description: The normalizeBaseUrl function is duplicated verbatim in two provider files: packages/stage-ui/src/stores/providers/unified/dashscope.ts (lines 5-9) and packages/stage-ui/src/stores/providers/unified/openrouter.ts (lines 8-12). The same logic also appears inline in the backend at services/airi-brain/src/adapters.ts (lines 16, 41, 90) as `baseURL.endsWith('/') ? baseURL : baseURL + '/'` and in embedding.ts (lines 63-65). This is the same URL normalization concern appearing in 4+ locations.

findings.title: Repeated dispose pattern across all module stores
findings.description: The exact same dispose() function pattern (iterate disposers array, call each, reset array) appears in 5 module stores: activity.ts (line 87-91), embedding.ts (line 85-89), llm.ts (line 85-89), memory.ts (line 180-184), and provider-sync.ts (line 113-117). All use `ref<Array<() => void>>([])` for disposers and the same cleanup loop. While each instance is only 5 lines, the pattern is used 5 times, totaling 25 lines of identical code.

findings.title: Duplicated LlmConfig and EmbeddingConfig types across store.ts and providers.ts
findings.description: The interfaces LlmConfig and EmbeddingConfig are defined identically in both services/airi-brain/src/store.ts (lines 19-24, 34-39) and services/airi-brain/src/providers.ts (lines 7-12, 14-19). Both files export these interfaces. This means there are 4 identical interface definitions (2 names x 2 files), all with the same 4 fields. The embedding handler imports from store.ts while providers.ts defines its own copies.

findings.title: Duplicated fetch request pattern in LLM adapter methods
findings.description: In services/airi-brain/src/adapters.ts, the createLlmProviderAdapter function has two methods (generateText lines 14-38, generateStructured lines 39-70) that share nearly identical fetch setup: extracting requestDefaults, normalizing baseURL, constructing URL, building headers, checking response.ok, extracting error body. The request setup + error handling accounts for ~15 lines duplicated between the two methods. The embedding adapter (lines 88-109) shares the same pattern too.

findings.title: setupApp message handler exceeds 100 lines with deep nesting
findings.description: The WebSocket message handler in packages/server-runtime/src/index.ts spans lines 198-413, approximately 215 lines. It contains a switch statement with 4 cases (heartbeat, authenticate, announce, ui:configure), followed by a default routing/broadcast section. The announce case (lines 264-306) has 4 levels of nesting (handler > switch > if > if). While each individual case is reasonable, the combined handler is a P1 due to exceeding 100 lines.

findings.title: Magic number 0.85 for dedup threshold in pipeline
findings.description: The dedup threshold 0.85 on line 89 of services/airi-brain/src/pipeline.ts is a magic number with no named constant. Per project convention, named constants are preferred over magic numbers.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
```ts
dedupThreshold: 0.85,
```

AFTER:
```ts
const DEFAULT_DEDUP_THRESHOLD = 0.85
// ...
dedupThreshold: DEFAULT_DEDUP_THRESHOLD,
```

WHY: Named constant communicates intent and makes the threshold discoverable for tuning. Consistent with the project's convention of named constants over magic numbers.

findings.title: Duplicate possibleEvents arrays in client and server channel store
findings.description: The possibleEvents array in packages/stage-ui/src/stores/mods/api/channel-server.ts (lines 23-83, ~40 entries) and services/airi-brain/src/index.ts (lines 68-114, ~40 entries) both list a largely overlapping set of event types. While they serve different roles (frontend vs backend), many event names are duplicated between them. If the event type system enforces a shared contract, these could be derived from a shared constant.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
```ts
// channel-server.ts: ~40 event names
// index.ts: ~40 event names (overlapping)
```

AFTER:
Consider defining shared event name groups in the server-shared or plugin-protocol package:
```ts
// server-shared/events.ts
export const BRAIN_EVENTS = [
  'memory:list', 'memory:search', ...
] as const
export const ACTIVITY_EVENTS = [...] as const
```

Then import and spread in both locations. Only do this if maintaining event parity between frontend and backend is important.

WHY: Reduces the risk of event names drifting between frontend and backend. The event types were already added to packages/plugin-protocol/src/types/events.ts in this diff, suggesting shared event typing is desired.

summary.verdict: The diff introduces substantial new functionality (Activity pipeline, LLM/Embedding config, provider sync, evening pipeline) but contains significant structural duplication. The most impactful issue is the P1 message handler in server-runtime at 215 lines. The dominant pattern across P2 findings is near-identical code between LLM and Embedding variants -- the frontend stores (llm.ts vs embedding.ts), backend handlers (llm.ts vs embedding.ts), provider handle factories (createLlmHandle vs createEmbeddingHandle), and type definitions (LlmConfig/EmbeddingConfig duplicated across files) all follow the same structure with minimal variation. Extracting shared utilities for: (1) provider credential resolution, (2) config completeness checking, (3) model handle creation, (4) error message extraction, and (5) disposer lifecycle management would eliminate the majority of duplication. The autoEnableModules function in onboarding.ts is also a straightforward data-driven refactor opportunity that would cut 68 lines to ~15.

positive_observations: Individual functions are generally short and well-scoped -- most are under 30 lines, consistent with project conventions.
Guard clauses and early returns are used effectively throughout (e.g., pipeline.ts checks for adapter availability, embedding.ts checks for config completeness before proceeding).
The pipeline architecture cleanly separates concerns: providers.ts for config, adapters.ts for protocol adaptation, pipeline.ts for orchestration, handlers/ for event routing.
Named constants are used for timing values (WINDOW_POLL_INTERVAL_MS, DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_TTL_MS, DEV_DEFAULT_WS), following project convention.
The BrainStore class in store.ts uses parameterized SQL queries throughout -- no string concatenation vulnerabilities.
